pi_items.source: WebFetch and WebSearch confirmed website exists and is publicly accessible; website contains test pages for SEO crawlers including mobile testing, meta tags, redirects, links, status codes, social tags, and content variations
Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 34, export_result.sh line 14, task_utils.sh lines 87 and 105 all reference this directory consistently as the target export location
WebSearch confirmed Screaming Frog SEO Spider supports CSV export with 55-65 columns; verifier.py and all scripts reference *.csv file pattern; export_result.sh processes CSV files at line 20

evidence_docs/README.md line 95 explicitly states 'At least 5 rows of data' in pass criteria; verifier.py line 70 implements check: csv_rows >= expected_min_rows (default 5); task description requires 'at least 5 rows of data'

privileged_info_summary: The task uses a real, publicly accessible website (https://crawler-test.com/) specifically designed for SEO crawler testing, not synthetic data. The Screaming Frog SEO Spider application (version 23.2) crawls this website and exports results as CSV files to /home/ga/Documents/SEO/exports/. The success criteria requires a minimum of 5 data rows in the CSV export, with strict verification that the exported data contains URLs from crawler-test.com to prevent false positives.
